config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society’s interests should automatically come before individual rights, because that can easily be used to justify abuse by governments or majorities. In my view, individual rights are the foundation that keeps society fair, especially for minorities and people without power. Society matters a lot, but its interests should be pursued within clear limits that protect basic personal freedoms.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat terlalu mudah bagi pemerintah atau mayoritas untuk memakai alasan “kepentingan umum” guna membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu pagar yang mencegah masyarakat berubah menjadi alat penindasan terhadap minoritas atau orang yang tidak populer. Tentu ada keadaan darurat seperti wabah atau keamanan publik, tetapi pembatasan harus sempit, sementara, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan aturan default. Kalau hak individu bisa disingkirkan kapan saja demi “masyarakat,” maka pada akhirnya tidak ada hak yang aman.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the idea that society should generally come before the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “the public interest” can be used to silence weak people or excuse unfair treatment. In practice, the majority often has more power, so if individual rights are not protected first, minorities and ordinary citizens can be pushed aside very quickly. That said, I do not believe rights are absolute in every situation; public health or safety can justify limited restrictions, but only when they are clear, necessary, and temporary. Society is strongest when it protects people, not when it demands that people sacrifice their rights by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena, dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, menempatkan kepentingan masyarakat di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum terlalu berbahaya. Pengalaman politik di Amerika membuat saya skeptis terhadap alasan “demi kepentingan umum,” karena itu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan oleh pemerintah atau mayoritas. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi dalam keadaan darurat, seperti wabah atau ancaman keamanan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, sementara, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan minoritas dan orang yang tidak punya kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective in Indonesia, the phrase “public interest” is too often used to justify pressure on weaker people, and once that happens, ordinary citizens and minorities usually pay the price. Society does need order and shared responsibility, but those goals should be limited by clear individual rights, not the other way around. In real life, a society is healthier when it protects people first and only restricts rights in narrow, necessary, and temporary cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena dari perspektif saya di Amerika, memberi prioritas pada kepentingan masyarakat di atas hak individu terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Poin tentang keadaan darurat memang valid, tetapi justru karena itu pembatasan harus benar-benar sempit, sementara, dan dapat diuji, bukan dijadikan alasan default. Saya juga tidak setuju bahwa masyarakat otomatis lebih penting daripada individu; menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dengan melindungi orang-orang yang paling rentan, bukan dengan mengorbankan hak mereka demi mayoritas. Jika hak individu bisa disisihkan kapan saja atas nama “kepentingan umum,” maka yang lemah akan selalu kalah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
